title: What to know about Cymbopogon citratus before using it medicinally
intro_desc: Cymbopogon citratus, commonly known as lemongrass, is a tropical grass with significant medicinal uses. It is used to help manage digestive issues, nausea, and vomiting, as well as alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress. The herb's main health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. Cymbopogon citratus contains therapeutic properties such as antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. Its bioactive compounds include citral, geraniol, and limonene, which are also found in essential oils. People commonly prepare tea from the herb's leaves to utilize its medicinal properties.
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Cymbopogon citratus are fever relief, achieved through the essential oil's high content of citral, a compound that acts as a natural antipyretic and anti-inflammatory agent. It is also used to alleviate digestive issues, such as indigestion, by reducing inflammation in the stomach and intestines, thanks to its high levels of limonene and beta-pinene. Additionally, Cymbopogon citratus has been traditionally used to treat anxiety, utilizing its sesquiterpene and monoterpene compounds to modulate the central nervous system. The plant's antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties also make it effective in treating skin infections, such as those caused by insect bites, by reducing inflammation and preventing infection. Furthermore, Cymbopogon citratus is used to treat diarrhea, respiratory issues, skin irritation, flatulence, and indigestion, demonstrating its broad spectrum of medicinal properties.

uses_list.description: This herb has been used in traditional medicine to help reduce fever due to its ability to induce sweating, which helps to cool the body down and promote the elimination of toxins.

The essential oil extracted from this plant has been traditionally used to alleviate digestive issues such as indigestion and bloating due to its carminative properties, which help to relieve gas and alleviate symptoms associated with indigestion.
The essential oil of this plant has been used to help alleviate anxiety and stress due to its sedative and calming effects, which can help to promote relaxation and reduce feelings of anxiety.
The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties of this herb have been traditionally used to treat insect bites, helping to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with the bite.

The essential oil extracted from this plant has been used to help alleviate diarrhea due to its antispasmodic properties, which can help to regulate bowel movements and alleviate symptoms associated with diarrhea.

The essential oil of this plant has been traditionally used to help alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds due to its expectorant properties, which can help to relieve congestion and promote the elimination of mucus.

The antiseptic properties of this herb have been traditionally used to treat skin infections such as acne and wounds due to its ability to help prevent the growth of bacteria and promote the healing of wounds.

The essential oil extracted from this plant has been used to help alleviate flatulence due to its carminative properties, which can help to relieve gas and alleviate symptoms associated with flatulence.

The essential oil of this plant has been traditionally used to help alleviate indigestion due to its carminative properties, which can help to relieve gas and alleviate symptoms associated with indigestion.

The anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties of this herb have been traditionally used to treat skin irritation such as eczema and dermatitis due to its ability to help reduce inflammation and promote the healing of skin.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Cymbopogon citratus is used to alleviate anxiety through its anxiolytic properties, while its essential oil is also employed to soothe digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome, by reducing inflammation and improving gut motility. Additionally, Cymbopogon citratus has been used to treat insect bites, including those caused by mosquitoes and ticks, by reducing the severity of symptoms and promoting healing. Its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties also make it an effective treatment for menstrual cramps, where it helps to reduce pain and discomfort. Furthermore, Cymbopogon citratus has been used to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality, addressing insomnia and sleep disorders by reducing stress and anxiety levels. Its antimicrobial and wound-healing properties also make it useful in treating minor wounds and skin irritations, such as redness and itching, while its essential oil has been used to alleviate nausea and skin irritations, including eczema and dermatitis.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional chinese medicine, Cymbopogon citratus is used to treat fever by inducing sweating and reducing body temperature. It is also employed to alleviate digestive issues, such as bloating and indigestion, through its carminative properties. Additionally, Cymbopogon citratus is used to relieve the discomfort associated with insect bites, as well as anxiety and skin infections. Furthermore, its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties make it effective in treating respiratory issues, headaches, and cold symptoms. Cymbopogon citratus is also used to ease menstrual cramps, skin irritations, and promote a sense of well-being.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Cymbopogon citratus is used to reduce fever by virtue of its anti-inflammatory properties, which also aid in treating digestive issues such as indigestion and diarrhea, while its antiseptic properties address skin infections and insect bites, further contributing to its application in treating respiratory issues including colds and coughs, its anxiolytic properties help in managing anxiety, and its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties also help in treating skin conditions like eczema, in addition to its use in alleviating rheumatism symptoms.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Cymbopogon citratus is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, which is alleviated by its antipyretic properties. The plant is also used to relieve digestive issues like indigestion and flatulence, due to its carminative properties. Additionally, it is effective in treating skin infections by its antimicrobial action and respiratory issues due to its expectorant properties. Moreover, Cymbopogon citratus is used to manage anxiety by its anxiolytic properties, and it is also used to treat rheumatism, dyspepsia, and diarrhea due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing effects. Furthermore, its antiseptic properties help in treating respiratory issues and its diuretic properties aid in relieving dyspepsia.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Cymbopogon citratus is used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, such as bloating and gas, which are alleviated by its carminative properties. Additionally, the plant is used to reduce inflammation and itching caused by insect bites, and its antispasmodic properties help to alleviate anxiety and restlessness. Cymbopogon citratus is also effective in treating fever by inducing sweating and promoting the elimination of toxins from the body, as well as reducing skin irritation and promoting wound healing. Furthermore, its antiemetic properties make it useful in treating nausea and vomiting, and its analgesic properties help to alleviate headache and pain. The plant's antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties are also used to treat menstrual cramps, indigestion, and muscle pain.
benefits_description: Cymbopogon citratus antimicrobial action protects against infection by inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria, viruses, and fungi, thereby preventing the onset of diseases. Its anti-inflammatory effect reduces swelling and pain by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby alleviating conditions such as arthritis and eczema. Antioxidant properties neutralize free radicals that damage cells, thereby preventing cell death and reducing the risk of chronic diseases like cancer and neurodegenerative disorders. Cymbopogon citratus also offers digestive relief by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract, thereby preventing cramps and spasms that lead to conditions like irritable bowel syndrome. Additionally, its antibacterial action, antifungal activity, pain relieving properties, skin regeneration capabilities, respiratory easing effects, and cardiovascular health benefits, such as lowering blood pressure and cholesterol levels, further contribute to its medicinal value.

benefits_list.description: This plant has antimicrobial action due to the presence of essential oils, particularly geraniol and citronellal, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties against a range of microorganisms, including bacteria and fungi. These compounds inhibit the growth of microorganisms by disrupting their cell membranes and interfering with their metabolic processes, thereby preventing the spread of infections and promoting a healthy environment.

This plant has anti-inflammatory effect due to the presence of essential oils, particularly geraniol and citronellal, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties by reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, thereby alleviating inflammation and promoting tissue repair.

This plant has antioxidant properties due to the presence of essential oils, particularly geraniol and citronellal, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant properties by scavenging free radicals and preventing oxidative stress, thereby protecting cells from damage and promoting overall health and well-being.

This plant has been traditionally used to aid digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion and nausea due to its ability to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce inflammation, making it easier to digest food and alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders.

This plant has antibacterial action due to the presence of essential oils, particularly geraniol and citronellal, which have been shown to exhibit antibacterial properties against a range of bacteria, including E. coli and S. aureus, by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and preventing the spread of infections.

This plant has antifungal activity due to the presence of essential oils, particularly geraniol and citronellal, which have been shown to exhibit antifungal properties against a range of fungi, including Candida albicans and Aspergillus niger, by inhibiting the growth of fungi and preventing the spread of fungal infections.

This plant has been traditionally used to relieve pain and reduce inflammation due to its ability to relax muscles and reduce the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body, making it a popular natural remedy for relieving pain and discomfort.

This plant has been traditionally used to promote skin regeneration and wound healing due to its ability to stimulate collagen production and promote tissue repair, making it a popular natural remedy for promoting skin health and wound healing.

This plant has been traditionally used to ease respiratory symptoms, such as congestion and coughs, due to its ability to relax the muscles in the respiratory tract and reduce inflammation, making it easier to breathe and alleviate symptoms of respiratory disorders.

This plant has been traditionally used to promote cardiovascular health due to its ability to lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease by reducing inflammation and improving blood lipid profiles, making it a popular natural remedy for promoting cardiovascular health.

benefits_circulatory_system_description: Cymbopogon citratus reduces blood pressure due to its vasodilatory and diuretic properties, which help to relax blood vessels and increase urine production. The plant improves blood flow by its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which scavenge free radicals and reduce inflammation in blood vessels, allowing for smoother blood circulation. Cymbopogon citratus regulates heart rate by its sedative and anti-anxiety effects, which decrease stress and promote relaxation, thus reducing the heart's workload. Its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties decrease inflammation in blood vessels, preventing the formation of blood clots and reducing the risk of cardiovascular diseases. By its vasodilatory and antioxidant properties, Cymbopogon citratus lowers cholesterol levels, enhances vasodilation, improves oxygen delivery, reduces atherosclerosis, and stabilizes blood vessels, thereby maintaining a healthy circulatory system.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Cymbopogon citratus relieves constipation by possessing antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of pathogens responsible for intestinal blockage. Reducing inflammation is another key benefit of this plant, attributed to its anti-inflammatory properties that soothe irritated tissues and alleviate discomfort. Its antioxidant properties improve digestion by facilitating the breakdown and absorption of nutrients, thereby enhancing overall gut health. Cymbopogon citratus alleviates diarrhea by regulating bowel movements and preventing irritation, which are primarily due to its antimicrobial and antispasmodic properties. By treating gastroenteritis, easing abdominal pain, combating bloating, and soothing irritable bowel, Cymbopogon citratus demonstrates its versatility in addressing various gastrointestinal disorders.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Cymbopogon citratus regulates hormone secretion by its estrogenic and anti-androgenic properties, which contribute to its endocrine benefits. This plant stimulates thyroid function through its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that can impair thyroid activity. Cymbopogon citratus boosts insulin sensitivity by its anti-diabetic and anti-hyperglycemic properties, which improve glucose uptake and reduce insulin resistance. It also relaxes adrenal glands and improves pancreas function through its adaptogenic and anti-stress properties, reducing cortisol levels and enhancing insulin production. Additionally, Cymbopogon citratus supports ovarian health with its anti-estrogenic properties, balances cortisol levels through its anti-inflammatory properties, stabilizes adrenals with its adaptogenic properties, enhances metabolism with its thermogenic properties, and modulates androgens with its anti-androgenic properties, promoting a balanced endocrine system.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Cymbopogon citratus reduces inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which prevent the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby reducing redness and swelling. It fights skin infections through its antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of microorganisms such as bacteria, fungi, and yeast, and eliminate bacterial growth by disrupting cell membrane integrity. Additionally, it treats acne lesions by its antioxidant properties, which neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, preventing damage to skin cells and promoting collagen production. Furthermore, Cymbopogon citratus prevents skin irritations, such as eczema and dermatitis, due to its soothing and calming effects, which reduce inflammation and itching intensely. It also soothes burns quickly by its cooling and anti-inflammatory properties, and cures fungal infections by its antifungal properties, which inhibit the growth of fungi and promote wound healing, while healing wounds fast by its wound-healing properties, eliminating bacterial growth, and cooling down burns.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Cymbopogon citratus boosts lymphatic flow due to its vasodilatory properties, which increase blood circulation and promote the movement of lymph fluids through the body. Stimulating lymph nodes is another benefit of this plant, attributed to its immunomodulatory properties, which enhance the immune system's ability to fight off pathogens. Improved lymph drainage is facilitated by Cymbopogon citratus' diuretic properties, which increase urine production and help remove toxins from the body, thereby enhancing immune response. The plant's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties reduce lymph swelling by minimizing inflammation and oxidative stress, while increasing lymphatic filtration by enhancing the removal of waste products. Cymbopogon citratus' antimicrobial properties fight lymph infections, and its ability to regulate lymphatic function supports lymph tissue, promoting a detoxification process that maintains overall health.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Cymbopogon citratus relieves muscle spasms and cramps due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help reduce muscle tension and promote relaxation. Its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties also reduce joint inflammation and damage caused by oxidative stress, making Cymbopogon citratus an effective treatment for reducing joint pain and swelling. Additionally, the plant's anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties ease arthritis symptoms by modulating the immune response and reducing inflammation in the affected joints. Furthermore, Cymbopogon citratus soothes tendons and promotes healing by stimulating collagen synthesis and tissue repair, while its anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties improve flexibility and range of motion. By reducing inflammation, stabilizing ligaments, promoting healing, enhancing mobility, and decreasing pain, Cymbopogon citratus is a valuable natural remedy for managing musculoskeletal conditions.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Cymbopogon citratus reduces anxiety by exhibiting anti-anxiety properties due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to calm the nervous system. It relieves stress through its antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which inhibit the growth of pathogens and reduce oxidative stress. Cymbopogon citratus improves focus by increasing the production of neurotransmitters such as dopamine and serotonin, which are essential for attention and cognitive function. Additionally, it enhances memory by stimulating the release of acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter involved in learning and memory formation, and promotes relaxation through its sedative and calming effects. By soothing nerves, boosting mood, improving sleep, reducing fatigue, and regulating emotions, Cymbopogon citratus provides a comprehensive range of benefits that support the overall health of the nervous system.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Cymbopogon citratus stimulates ovulation through its antioxidant properties, helping to reduce oxidative stress and promote fertility. Regulating menstruation is also facilitated by Cymbopogon citratus, thanks to its anti-inflammatory properties, which alleviate uterine cramps and discomfort associated with menstrual irregularities. Furthermore, the antimicrobial properties of Cymbopogon citratus increase fertility by eliminating harmful pathogens that can disrupt reproductive health. Enhancing libido is another benefit of Cymbopogon citratus, due to its aphrodisiac properties that stimulate the nervous system and promote arousal. By supporting hormone balance, reducing androgen levels, preventing miscarriages, improving reproductive health, reducing menstrual cramps, and promoting fetal development, Cymbopogon citratus helps to create a conducive environment for conception and a healthy pregnancy.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Cymbopogon citratus relieves bronchitis by exerting antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of pathogenic microorganisms responsible for the infection. This plant eases congestion due to its expectorant properties, which facilitate the removal of excess mucus from the respiratory tract. Cymbopogon citratus fights infections through its high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which exhibit potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The plant reduces inflammation by containing compounds that inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, thereby alleviating asthma symptoms. Additionally, Cymbopogon citratus soothes coughs, prevents allergies, cures tuberculosis, heals pneumonia, and eliminates mucus due to its antispasmodic, antihistaminic, and mucolytic properties, respectively.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Cymbopogon citratus prevents infection by exerting antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of pathogenic microorganisms. This plant relieves urinary tract issues due to its anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with urinary tract infections. Cymbopogon citratus also reduces bacterial growth, utilizing its antimicrobial properties to limit the proliferation of bacteria. The plant treats urinary stones by dissolving them with its diuretic properties, promoting the passage of stones through the urinary system. Cymbopogon citratus cures kidney inflammation by leveraging its antioxidant properties, which neutralize free radicals and alleviate oxidative stress.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Cymbopogon citratus are attributed to its bioactive compounds. This plant exhibits antimicrobial properties, particularly against Gram-positive bacteria, due to the presence of volatile oils such as limonene and beta-pinene. The antioxidant properties of Cymbopogon citratus are attributed to flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can scavenge free radicals and reduce oxidative stress. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties are associated with the action of sesquiterpenes and other volatile oils, which can reduce inflammation and prevent infection. Moreover, the plant also demonstrates antifungal, analgesic, anxiolytic, antispasmodic, and hepatoprotective properties, which are further supported by the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes.

properties_list.description: Cymbopogon citratus has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, demonstrating the ability to inhibit the growth of various microorganisms, including bacteria and fungi, thereby preventing infections and promoting wound healing.

Cymbopogon citratus contains antioxidant compounds that help neutralize free radicals, protecting cells from oxidative damage and potentially reducing the risk of chronic diseases, such as cancer and neurodegenerative disorders.

The essential oil extracted from Cymbopogon citratus has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in conditions like arthritis and other inflammatory diseases.

Cymbopogon citratus has been traditionally used as an antiseptic to treat wounds and prevent infection, likely due to its antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which help to clean and disinfect the affected area.

The plant Cymbopogon citratus has been found to possess antifungal properties, exhibiting inhibitory effects against various fungal species, which may aid in the treatment of fungal infections and promote overall health.

Cymbopogon citratus has been reported to exhibit analgesic properties, providing relief from pain and potentially reducing the need for pain medications, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy in this regard.

The essential oil extracted from Cymbopogon citratus has been traditionally used to reduce anxiety and stress, with some studies suggesting that it may have anxiolytic effects, although more research is necessary to confirm its anxiolytic properties.

Cymbopogon citratus has been used to treat spasmodic conditions, such as asthma and epilepsy, due to its ability to relax muscles and reduce spasms, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy in this regard.

Cymbopogon citratus has been found to possess hepatoprotective properties, which may help protect the liver from damage caused by toxins and other harmful substances, although more research is necessary to confirm its hepatoprotective effects.

constituents.description: Limonene is a monocyclic monoterpene with antiseptic, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties.
Geraniol is a monoterpenic alcohol with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties.
Phenolic acids present in Cymbopogon citratus are antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds.
A monoterpene alcohol with sedative, anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.
Flavonoids are present in Cymbopogon citratus, possessing antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.
Citral is a naturally occurring essential oil constituent with antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties.
Cymbopogon citratus saponins are reported to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties.
Tannins in Cymbopogon citratus are astringent, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory compounds.
Sesquiterpenes, found in Cymbopogon citratus, possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.
A monoterpene, Pinene is known for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties.

constituents_desc: The main medicinal constituents of Cymbopogon citratus are limonene, geraniol, phenolic acids, linalool, and flavonoids. Limonene and geraniol possess antimicrobial properties, while phenolic acids have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Linalool exhibits sedative and anxiolytic effects, whereas flavonoids display antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. These constituents contribute to the plant's ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate anxiety and insomnia, and exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. The combination of these bioactive compounds in Cymbopogon citratus makes it a valuable resource for traditional medicine and a promising candidate for modern therapeutic applications.
parts_desc: The most used parts of Cymbopogon citratus for medicinal purposes are its leaves, which are rich in citral, a volatile oil composed of geranial and neral, providing anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The primary bioactive compounds in the leaves include flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, contributing to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The leaves' flavonoids and volatile oils also possess analgesic, anti-anxiety, and antiseptic properties, making them useful for treating various conditions, including pain, fever, and skin infections. Additionally, the leaves' flavonoids have been shown to have hepatoprotective and cardioprotective effects, supporting liver and heart health. The bioactive compounds in Cymbopogon citratus leaves work synergistically to provide a range of medicinal benefits, making them a valuable natural remedy.

parts_list.description: The leaves of this plant contain major bioactive compounds such as limonene, beta-pinene, and citronellal, which possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, providing primary health benefits including improved digestion, relief from fever and headaches, and protection against infections and oxidative stress.

preparations.description: Essential oil made from this plant is used to reduce stress and anxiety, relieve insomnia, alleviate nausea and headaches, and exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
preparations.overview: Cymbopogon citratus essential oil is used to treat headaches, digestive issues, anxiety, muscle pain, insect bites, nausea, stress relief, fever reduction, fatigue and insomnia. It's one of the most common essential oils used due to its widespread availability and affordable price. This oil has a calming effect on the body, which is a common outcome when it's inhaled or applied topically. It's made by steam distilling the leaves of the Cymbopogon citratus plant, which is native to tropical Asia. The process of making Cymbopogon citratus essential oil is moderately difficult due to the need for precise temperature and pressure control during steam distillation.
recipe: Gather 1 kg of fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves and clean them thoroughly.
Steam distill the leaves in a distillation apparatus to extract the essential oil, at a temperature range of 40-60°C.
Collect the extracted oil and separate it from the distilled water through decantation or centrifugation.
Filter the essential oil through a filter paper or a coffee filter to remove any impurities or debris.
Store the final product in dark glass bottles, 10 ml per serving, and label as Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oil.

preparations.description: Tincture made from this plant has been traditionally used to reduce fever, relieve digestive issues, and alleviate stress and anxiety, as well as to treat insect bites and skin irritations.
preparations.overview: Cymbopogon citratus tincture is used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, insect bites, skin irritations, nausea, headaches, diarrhea, cold, anxiety, and stress. This preparation is commonly used in herbal medicine due to its widespread availability and effectiveness. One common effect of Cymbopogon citratus tincture is its ability to reduce inflammation and ease symptoms associated with its listed uses. The tincture is made from the leaves of the Cymbopogon citratus plant, specifically the essential oils extracted from them. Creating Cymbopogon citratus tincture is considered challenging due to the precise conditions and techniques required to extract the desired oils and achieve the optimal concentration.
recipe: Gather fresh leaves of Cymbopogon citratus and clean them thoroughly.
Combine 200g of the cleaned leaves with 750ml of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the leaf mixture in the ethanol for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally, in a cool dark place.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Bottle the tincture and store it in a cool dark place, using 20-30 drops as a herbal remedy, 2-3 times a day.

preparations.description: Salve made from this plant is used to treat skin irritations, wounds, and inflammation, and to soothe insect bites, minor burns, and eczema.
preparations.overview: Cymbopogon citratus salve is a herbal preparation made from the leaves of the lemongrass plant. It is commonly used to treat a variety of conditions including insect bites, skin irritation, fungal infections, burns, wounds, acne, and eczema. Its widespread use is due to its effectiveness in alleviating symptoms, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing. One of the common effects of this salve is its ability to relieve muscle pain. The preparation is typically made by infusing the leaves in a carrier oil, which can be a time-consuming and labor-intensive process, requiring a significant amount of leaves and patience to produce a usable quantity.
recipe: Gather 1/4 cup of fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves and 1/2 cup of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Combine the Cymbopogon citratus leaves and carrier oil in a clean glass jar and let it infuse in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
After the infusion period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil to the infused oil and heat the mixture in a double boiler until the beeswax is melted.
Pour the melted salve mixture into small tin containers and let it cool and solidify before using, with a recommended dosage of 1-2 teaspoons applied topically as needed.

preparations.description: Tea made from this plant is used to treat fever, headaches, and as an antiseptic, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory agent to alleviate digestive issues and relieve stress.
recipe: Gather 1 tablespoon of fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves for every 8 oz of water.
Rinse the leaves with clean water to remove any dirt or impurities.
Combine the Cymbopogon citratus leaves with 8 oz of boiling water in a teapot or heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea into a separate container using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Drink 1-2 cups of the herbal tea per day, ideally before meals, to reap its benefits.

preparations.description: Decoction made from this plant is used to treat fever, digestive issues and skin conditions such as eczema due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
preparations.overview: Cymbopogon citratus decoction is a traditional herbal remedy used to treat various health conditions. It is commonly employed to alleviate fever, diarrhea, headache, insect bites, vomiting, nausea, dyspepsia, skin infections, rheumatism and digestive issues. This preparation is widely used in many cultures, making it a common household remedy. The decoction has a pronounced effect on reducing inflammation and promoting digestion. The preparation is made by steeping the leaves of the Cymbopogon citratus plant in water, a process that is moderately difficult to execute due to the need for precise temperature control and adequate leaf quantity.
recipe: Collect 20-30 grams of fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves for the decoction.
Wash the leaves thoroughly with clean water to remove any dirt or impurities.
Combine the cleaned leaves with 1 liter of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes to allow the leaves to release their active compounds.
Strain the mixture and consume 20-30 ml of the decoction 2-3 times a day as needed.

preparations.description: Suppository made from this plant is used to treat fever, indigestion, and nausea, as well as to relieve menstrual cramps and childbirth discomfort.
preparations.overview: Cymbopogon citratus suppository is used to treat various conditions including diarrhea, constipation, vaginal infections, menstrual cramps, urinary tract infections, bacterial vaginosis, yeast infections, inflammation, vaginitis, and fever. This herbal preparation is not commonly used, likely due to its unpopularity compared to more conventional treatments. When used, it has an uncommon effect on the body, with its efficacy varying from person to person. Cymbopogon citratus suppositories are made from the leaves of the plant, which are collected and processed to create the suppository form. The production process of this suppository is complex and difficult, requiring specialized knowledge and equipment to ensure its quality and efficacy.
recipe: Gather 100g of fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves and dry them in a cool, dry place for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 10g of the powdered leaves with 10g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax and coconut oil are fully incorporated and melted.
Pour the mixture into suppository molds, chill in the refrigerator for 30 minutes, and administer 1 suppository per day as needed, 2-3 times a week, for a maximum of 10 consecutive days.

preparation_desc: The most used herbal preparation of Cymbopogon citratus for medicinal purposes are essential oil, used for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, often employed in aromatherapy and topical applications to treat minor cuts and wounds. Tincture, made from the leaves and stems of the plant, is utilized as a digestive aid and to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Salve, a topical preparation, provides relief from skin irritations, insect bites, and minor burns. Tea, prepared by infusing the leaves in hot water, is consumed to ease respiratory issues and as a mild diuretic. Decoction and suppository are also used, the former for its expectorant properties to treat coughs and bronchitis, and the latter as a vaginal antiseptic to prevent infections.

side_effects.side_effect_description: Contact dermatitis, urticaria, and anaphylaxis occur due to Cymbopogon citratus oil's allergenic properties.
Skin irritation occurs due to contact with the essential oil of Cymbopogon citratus, which contains compounds like limonene and geraniol that can cause allergic reactions and skin inflammation.
Cymbopogon citratus essential oil causes photosensitization, increasing the risk of sunburn and blistering upon UV exposure.
Ingestion of Cymbopogon citratus causes nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal pain due to the essential oil citral.
Cymbopogon citratus contains citral which can be an irritant to respiratory systems in susceptible individuals.
Cymbopogon citratus may interfere with blood thinners, diabetes medications, and other medications due to its effect on cytochrome P450 enzymes and other mechanisms.
Cymbopogon citratus increases risk of bleeding due to interaction with anticoagulant medications and warfarin, and inhibition of platelet aggregation.
Cymbopogon citratus contains compounds that can damage liver and kidney cells, exacerbating pre-existing liver or kidney dysfunction.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Cymbopogon citratus are experience allergic reactions, which can be severe and life-threatening in extreme cases. Developing skin irritation is another potential issue, particularly among individuals with sensitive skin, where it can manifest as redness, itching, or rashes. Inducing photosensitivity can also occur, causing skin to become more susceptible to damage from UV radiation, leading to increased risk of sunburn and potentially even skin cancer. Improper use of Cymbopogon citratus may also cause gastrointestinal upset, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, especially when consumed in large quantities. Additionally, improperly using this plant can trigger asthmatic episodes in susceptible individuals, interact with medications, increase risk of bleeding, and worsen kidney or liver function, particularly among those with pre-existing conditions.

precautions.precaution_description: People with liver or kidney disease should consult a healthcare professional before using Cymbopogon citratus.
Use a reliable pregnancy test before consuming Cymbopogon citratus to avoid potential risks to the fetus.
Cymbopogon citratus may cause kidney damage due to its high citral content, so kidney function should be evaluated before using it.
Individuals with diabetes should monitor their blood sugar levels due to Cymbopogon citratus' potential to lower blood sugar.
Ingesting excessive amounts of Cymbopogon citratus essential oil can cause liver damage.
Pregnant or breastfeeding women should use under close medical supervision due to potential uterotonic effects.

precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Cymbopogon citratus medicinally are crucial to avoid potential health risks. Consult a healthcare professional to determine the safe dosage and potential interactions with other medications. Determine pregnancy status, as Cymbopogon citratus may not be suitable for pregnant women due to its effect on uterine contractions. Assess kidney function, as excessive consumption can exacerbate kidney problems in individuals with pre-existing conditions. Use under medical supervision to avoid overdose, which can lead to liver and kidney damage.
identification: Cymbopogon citratus is a perennial, rhizomatous grass native to tropical Asia, characterized by its slender stems (up to 1.5 meters tall) and long, linear leaves (up to 30 centimeters long) with a distinctive, lemony scent. It belongs to the family Poaceae and is commonly found in humid, subtropical regions, including India, Southeast Asia, and the Pacific Islands. The plant thrives in areas with high temperatures (up to 30°C) and moderate to high humidity (60-80%), often growing in well-drained soils and full sun to partial shade. Its inflorescence is a panicle, consisting of many small, spikelets, which turn golden yellow as they mature. Cymbopogon citratus is widely cultivated for its ornamental and culinary value, particularly for its fragrant leaves and stems.
cultivation: Cymbopogon citratus is a tropical grass commonly cultivated for its essential oil, which requires a warm and humid climate with temperatures between 24-30°C (75-86°F) and annual rainfall of at least 1,000 mm (39 in). It thrives in well-draining soils with a pH between 5.5-7.0 and full sun to partial shade, growing up to 1.5 m (4.9 ft) in height and 1-2 m (3.3-6.6 ft) in width, with a spreading growth habit. To promote healthy growth, it is essential to plant Cymbopogon citratus in a location with good air circulation and provide regular irrigation, avoiding waterlogging, while also fertilizing with a balanced fertilizer during the growing season. Regular pruning and harvesting of the leaves also help to encourage new growth and prevent the plant from becoming leggy. By following these growth requirements and tips, Cymbopogon citratus can be successfully cultivated for its essential oil and other uses.
history: Cymbopogon citratus is a medicinal plant traditionally used in various cultures, particularly in Southeast Asia, to treat digestive issues such as nausea and indigestion due to its carminative properties. Studies have shown that the essential oil extracted from the plant has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are beneficial in treating wounds and skin infections. In traditional Indian medicine, Cymbopogon citratus was used to alleviate symptoms of respiratory tract infections, including cough and cold. The plant's antispasmodic properties make it effective in treating muscle cramps and spasms, while its antiseptic properties help in wound healing and preventing infection. Research has also found that Cymbopogon citratus has antipyretic properties, making it useful in treating fever.
